title: Boolean Algebra Basics: Security System Design Scenario
description: Progressive learning of Boolean algebra concepts through security system design role-play
scenario: You are a security system engineer who needs to design an access control system for a building
learningObjectives: Understand the application of Boolean algebra in real-world scenarios
Master the functions of AND, OR, NOT logic gates
Be able to construct and analyze simple truth tables

interactiveModules.title: Security System Design Scenario
interactiveModules.learningObjective: Understand the application of Boolean algebra in real-world scenarios
interactiveModules.content: You are designing an access control system for a building. Only authorized personnel can enter sensitive areas.

dialogue.introduction: Welcome to the security system design project. As a security engineer, your task is to design a reliable access control system.
dialogue.exploration: The building has different areas: public area, office area, and server room. We need to set appropriate access control for each area.

branches.prompt: Which logic gate would you choose to implement "only swiping card AND entering correct password can enter the server room"?

options.feedback: Correct! AND gate requires both inputs to be true before outputting true, perfectly matching the "swipe card AND enter password" requirement.
OR gate outputs true when any input is true, which means just swiping card OR entering password allows entry, not meeting security requirements.
options.remedialContent: Let's review: AND gate requires all conditions to be met simultaneously, OR gate requires only one condition to be met.

options.feedback: NOT gate is an inversion operation, not suitable for this scenario.
options.remedialContent: NOT gate has only one input, outputting the opposite of the input. It's used to invert signals, not to combine conditions.

dialogue.conclusion: Well done! You've mastered the application of AND gate in security systems. Now let's continue designing access control for other areas.

interactiveModules.title: Knowledge Checkpoint: AND Gate Truth Table
interactiveModules.content: Test your understanding of AND gate function

quiz.questionStem: For AND gate, when input A=1, B=0, what is the output?

feedback.correct: Correct! AND gate outputs 1 only when all inputs are 1.
feedback.incorrect: Think again, AND gate outputs 1 only when all inputs are 1.

progressiveHints.text: Recall the definition of AND gate: output is true only when all inputs are true.
AND gate is equivalent to logical "AND" operation, requiring all conditions to be met simultaneously.
